item.title: The Revolutionary Gordon Wood
item.description: The historian who recovered the radicalism of the American founding died on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Craig Bruce Smith

item.title: Actually, the SAT Was Necessary After All
item.description: University of California faculty are in open revolt over the lack of standardized test scores.
item.dc:creator: Idrees Kahloon

item.title: Why This Summer’s Airfares Are Unaffordable—And Unpredictable
item.description: The normal patterns of price changes may no longer hold true.
item.dc:creator: Henry Grabar

item.title: What Donald Trump Will Never Understand About Fighting
item.description: Mixed martial arts has a lot to teach the president.
item.dc:creator: Bhumika Tharoor

item.title: Being Black in Pete Hegseth’s Military
item.description: The secretary of defense is sending the message that Black service members are not welcome.
item.dc:creator: Clint Smith

item.title: The Americans Shelling Out Five Figures for a Coat of Arms
item.description: A nation founded on the rejection of aristocratic traditions still yearns for them.
item.dc:creator: Helen Lewis

item.title: Why Republicans Aren’t Condemning Trump’sMeet the PressWalkout
item.description: Denying the legitimacy of vote-counting has become party doctrine.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: The White Identitarians Are Having a Moment
item.description: Now that DEI and anti-racism are in retreat, they’re moving on to a more ambitious goal.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Chatterton Williams

item.title: The ‘Battle Hymn’ Can’t Be Ignored
item.description: The rousing urgency of the song seems to possess a power that, according to its own lyrics, “transfigures you and me.”
item.dc:creator: Jake Lundberg

item.title: How America Gave Up on Its Own History
item.description: Unable to agree on how to interpret the American story, the country’s schools, universities, and political institutions have stopped trying to tell it at all.
item.dc:creator: Yoni Appelbaum
